description.task: Implement a TwoPointers class with a method that finds two elements in a sorted array that sum to a given target using the two-pointer technique (O(n) time). Also implement a method to find all such pairs.
instructions: findPair: set left=0, right=arr.length-1; while left<right: if arr[left]+arr[right]==target return {left,right}; if sum<target left++; else right--;
findAllPairs: same two-pointer; on match add {left,right} then left++ and right-- to continue
For arr=[1,2,3,4,6,8,9] and target=10: first pair is indices 1 (value 2) and 5 (value 8), wait arr[1]+arr[5]=2+8=10 ✓
description.hint: int left=0,right=sortedArr.length-1; while(left<right){ int s=sortedArr[left]+sortedArr[right]; if(s==target) return new int[]{left,right}; else if(s<target) left++; else right--; }
description.expectedOutput:
Pair summing to 10: [1, 5]
Pair summing to 20: [-1, -1]
All pairs summing to 8:
  [0, 6]
  [1, 5]
  [2, 4]

description.task: Implement a SlidingWindow class with a method to find the maximum sum of any k consecutive elements using a sliding window (O(n) time), and a method to count subarrays whose sum equals a given target using a HashMap-based prefix sum approach.
instructions: maxSumWindow: after computing first window sum, loop i from k to arr.length-1; windowSum += arr[i] - arr[i-k]; update maxSum

countSubarraysWithSum: maintain a running prefixSum and a HashMap<Integer,Integer> freq; initialize freq.put(0,1); for each element add to prefixSum, count += freq.getOrDefault(prefixSum-target,0), then increment freq for prefixSum
description.hint: maxSumWindow sliding: for(int i=k;i<arr.length;i++){ windowSum+=arr[i]-arr[i-k]; if(windowSum>maxSum) maxSum=windowSum; } return maxSum;

description.task: Implement a PrefixSum class that builds a prefix sum array and uses it to answer range sum queries in O(1) time. Also implement a method to find the equilibrium index (where left sum equals right sum).
instructions: Constructor: prefix[0]=0; for i in 1..arr.length: prefix[i] = prefix[i-1] + arr[i-1]
equilibriumIndex: for each i, check if prefix[i] == prefix[n] - prefix[i+1]; return i if true, -1 if none found
description.hint: rangeSum: return prefix[right+1]-prefix[left]; equilibrium: int n=arr.length; for(int i=0;i<n;i++) if(prefix[i]==prefix[n]-prefix[i+1]) return i; return -1;
